_README: LLM-derived 0–3 axis scores for vermouth + quinquina-family aperitif wines (Lillet, Byrrh, Dubonnet, Cocchi Americano). Axes: sweet, bitter, citrus, herbal, floral, fruited. `bitter` separates Punt e Mes (3, half-amaro) from regular sweet vermouth (1). `floral` separates blanc/rosé styles (Pretty Blanc 3, Lillet Rosé 3, Cinzano Bianco 3) from reds. `citrus` separates Lillet Blanc / Cocchi Americano (3) from Italian sweets. Re-score after tasting.

239.name: Carpano Antica Formula Vermouth
239.reasoning: Italian sweet (1786, the original). Alpine wormwood + Madagascar vanilla + saffron. Big-bodied vanilla/spice character; clearly sweeter and more complex than Dolin Rouge. 16.5% ABV.

240.name: Dolin Blanc Vermouth
240.reasoning: French blanc (Chambéry AOC) with 32-33 botanicals. Lighter sweet with citrus + floral character. The French blanc style is brighter than Italian bianco.

156.name: Dolin Dry Vermouth
156.reasoning: French dry (Chambéry AOC), 30+ Alpine herbs, 17.5% ABV. The benchmark dry: crisp, low-sweet, herbal-citrus.

508.name: Dolin Rouge Vermouth
508.reasoning: French sweet vermouth (Savoie). Producer notes: 'lighter and more delicate than many Italian sweet vermouths, with herbal and floral notes, gentle sweetness, clean finish.' Notably less heavy than Carpano.

135.name: Punt e Mes
135.reasoning: Italian vermouth-bitter hybrid (1870, Piedmont). Name = 'one and a half points' (sweet + half bitter). The benchmark for high-bitter vermouth — sits between sweet vermouth and amaro.

582.name: Cinzano Bianco
582.reasoning: Italian bianco (Turin, 1757). Producer notes: 'floral and vanilla notes.' Italian-style bianco is sweeter and more floral than the French blanc.

600.name: BroVo Witty Dry Vermouth
600.reasoning: BroVo's dry vermouth (Woodinville, WA), 17% ABV, 90 pts Wine Enthusiast. Thin description; scored as a high-quality American dry vermouth — crisp, herbal-citrus, low-sweet.

601.name: BroVo Pretty Blanc Vermouth
601.reasoning: BroVo's 'blanc floral vermouth' — name + product family signals floral-forward blanc. 17% ABV.

602.name: BroVo Jammy Sweet Vermouth
602.reasoning: BroVo's sweet red — 'Jammy' name signals fruit-forward register vs Carpano's vanilla-spice register. Platinum 96 pts BTI.

603.name: BroVo Pink Rosé Vermouth
603.reasoning: BroVo's rosé vermouth, 17% ABV, Gold 93 pts BTI. Floral + light-fruit profile typical of rosé vermouths.

352.name: Lillet Blanc
352.reasoning: French quinquina-style aperitif wine (Bordeaux). Description is auto-stub so scored from canonical knowledge: bright citrus (orange peel, candied grapefruit), light honey, modest floral, low bitter (the modern Lillet Blanc lost much of its quinine vs the original Kina Lillet).

572.name: Lillet Rosé
572.reasoning: French rosé aperitif. Producer notes: 'delicate fruit and floral notes.' Rosé expression of Lillet — softer citrus than blanc, more floral and fruit.

541.name: Byrrh Grand Quinquina
541.reasoning: French aromatized wine, 18% ABV. Producer notes: 'quinine, coffee, cocoa, bitter orange. Rich, bittersweet, wine-forward.' Heavier and more bittersweet than Dubonnet; coffee/cocoa register is distinctive.

548.name: Dubonnet Rouge
548.reasoning: French aromatized wine, 14.8% ABV. Producer notes: 'sweet, fruity, gently bitter.' Sweeter and less bitter than Byrrh; softer profile.

527.name: Cocchi Americano
527.reasoning: Italian aperitif wine (Asti) — Moscato d'Asti + herbs + cinchona (quinine). Canonical Kina Lillet substitute for Vespers. Mid-bitter (cinchona is real), bright Moscato citrus, herbal complexity.
